# UC-12.7.15 · Control-M Server and Gateway Resource Saturation — DB Connections and Thread Pools

## Description

The Control-M server tier collapses under connection leaks, JDBC pool exhaustion, and JVM thread starvation long before jobs fail visibly. Mining alert and audit signatures for database and thread pressure gives platform owners a leading indicator.

## Value

Avoids enterprise-wide scheduling brownouts during month-end peaks and shortens war-room time when EM is unstable.

## Implementation

Normalize `signature` with `rex` or `eval` to strip timestamps. Enrich with DB AWR snapshots via DB Connect for correlation. Route critical sev to platform NOC. Tune noise with allowlists for known maintenance scripts.

## SPL

```spl
index=batch sourcetype="controlm:alert" OR sourcetype="controlm:audit"
| search "connection pool" OR "thread pool" OR "out of memory" OR "database" OR "too many connections" OR "ORA-00020" OR "SQL*Net"
| eval sev=case(match(_raw,"(?i)critical|severe"),"critical", match(_raw,"(?i)warn"),"warning", 1=1,"info")
| stats count, latest(_time) as last_evt, values(controlm_server) as servers by signature, sev
| where count>=3
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Timechart of alert rate by signature, gauge (open critical), table with server and DBA owner.
